findings.claim: Android 15 (API 35) introduces a 6-hour cumulative runtime limit per 24-hour rolling window for dataSync and mediaProcessing foreground service types, but the 'microphone' type is EXPLICITLY EXEMPT from this timeout.

Apps targeting Android 15 are prohibited from launching a 'microphone' foreground service directly from a BOOT_COMPLETED broadcast receiver. This prevents silent recording on device startup without user interaction.

Android 15 narrows the SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW exemption: merely holding the permission no longer allows background FGS starts. The app must have a CURRENTLY VISIBLE overlay window (TYPE_APPLICATION_OVERLAY) on screen to qualify for the exemption.

PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK within a foreground service keeps the CPU running with the screen off. Standard apps have wake locks ignored during Doze mode, but apps on the battery optimization whitelist (Unrestricted mode) retain PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK and network access even during deep Doze.

Android Vitals flags apps holding non-exempt partial wake locks for >2 cumulative hours in 24 hours with screen off. However, wake locks created by the system on behalf of audio playback/recording (tags containing '*audio*') are EXEMPT from this 2-hour calculation. Manual wake locks with custom tags WILL count.

To request Unrestricted battery mode programmatically, declare REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS permission and use ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS intent with package URI. On Android 12+, accepting moves the app to STANDBY_BUCKET_EXEMPTED (bucket value 5). Google Play restricts this permission to apps whose core functionality is severely impacted by battery optimizations.

CompanionDeviceManager's REQUEST_COMPANION_START_FOREGROUND_SERVICES_FROM_BACKGROUND permission does NOT allow starting 'microphone' type services from the background. The 'microphone' type requires 'while-in-use' status (app in foreground). A SecurityException is thrown even with the companion permission. Only types without while-in-use constraints (connectedDevice, dataSync, mediaPlayback, health) are allowed.

ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException is thrown when: (1) no foregroundServiceType declared in manifest (Android 14+), (2) attempting to start FGS from background without exemption, (3) exceeding 6-hour timeout for dataSync/mediaProcessing, (4) starting restricted types from BOOT_COMPLETED. Production pattern: wrap startForegroundService() in try-catch and fall back to WorkManager expedited work.

Android 14+ requires foregroundServiceType in manifest and type-specific permissions (FOREGROUND_SERVICE + FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MICROPHONE). The type must also be passed explicitly to startForeground() via ServiceInfo.FOREGROUND_SERVICE_TYPE_MICROPHONE or the app crashes with SecurityException.

Android 12+ (API 31) background start restrictions treat system-initiated service restarts (START_STICKY) as background starts. Unless the app qualifies for an exemption at the exact moment of restart, the system may block the restart or throw ForegroundServiceStartNotAllowedException. Returning START_NOT_STICKY is safer to prevent null-intent crashes.

Android 13+ Task Manager allows users to stop any foreground service from the notification drawer. Once stopped this way, the service will NOT restart regardless of START_STICKY or START_REDELIVER_INTENT flags.

Android 15 requires apps to be the top-visible app or have an active foreground service to successfully call requestAudioFocus(). Background apps without an active FGS will have audio focus requests silently denied.

Samsung One UI 7 uses Galaxy AI predictive engine to pre-kill services: if it predicts the user won't open an app for 4+ hours, it may terminate background FGS regardless of battery settings. The only reliable workaround is adding the app to 'Never sleeping apps' (Settings > Battery > Background usage limits).

Xiaomi HyperOS 2 requires explicit 'Background Autostart' permission (proprietary AppOps code 10008) for services to restart after kill or reboot. Without it, the service remains dead indefinitely. The 'Padlock' icon in Recents is the only way to prevent the Deep Clean cycle from killing the process.

WorkManager heartbeat/watchdog pattern: a PeriodicWorkRequest (minimum 15-minute interval) checks a static isRunning flag and calls startForegroundService() if the service died. On Android 12+, the WorkRequest must use setExpedited() to gain background FGS start privileges, but expedited job quotas may be exhausted.

Google Play requires a formal declaration for microphone FGS under Policy > App content, including feature description, user impact statement, and video demonstration showing the user journey that triggers the microphone service. Developer verification deadline: October 30, 2025.

The Doze whitelist (ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS) is the safer programmatic approach. ACTION_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATION_SETTINGS (no special permission required) opens the full settings list for manual user selection, avoiding Play Store rejection risk.

App Standby Buckets can be queried via UsageStatsManager.appStandbyBucket: STANDBY_BUCKET_EXEMPTED (5) = Unrestricted, ACTIVE (10) through RARE (40) = Optimized, STANDBY_BUCKET_RESTRICTED (45) = Restricted. Once set to Unrestricted, the app is exempt from most background limitations and Doze mode.
Android 15 Doze mode activates 50% faster than previous versions, making battery whitelist exemption even more critical for always-on services.

If a service declares multiple types (e.g., microphone|dataSync), the dataSync portion is still subject to the 6-hour limit while the microphone portion remains exempt. Mixed-type services require careful architecture.

key_insights: A microphone-type foreground service is the golden path for always-on recording: it is exempt from the 6-hour timeout, and system-managed audio wake locks are exempt from the 2-hour Android Vitals penalty. The critical constraint is that it MUST be started while the app is in the foreground (no background start, no boot start, no CompanionDeviceManager bypass for microphone type).

The battery optimization whitelist (Unrestricted/Exempted bucket) is essential: it grants Doze-mode wake lock persistence, background start exemption, and network access during deep Doze. Request via ACTION_REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS with a compelling UX explanation, but also implement the manual settings fallback (ACTION_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATION_SETTINGS) to avoid Play Store rejection.

OEM-specific killing (Samsung Galaxy AI predictive killing, Xiaomi HyperOS autostart gate) cannot be solved programmatically. The app MUST include an onboarding flow that guides users through device-specific settings (Samsung: Never sleeping apps; Xiaomi: Background Autostart + Padlock). A WorkManager watchdog every 15 minutes provides a safety net but cannot guarantee immediate restart on Android 12+.
gaps: Exact behavior of Android 16 (API 36) runtime quotas for jobs triggered from foreground services -- this was mentioned in previews but detailed documentation is not yet available.

Whether the VoiceInteractionService (Digital Assistant role) path would survive all the same restrictions as a standard microphone FGS -- this is the only official 'always-on listening' API but requires being selected as Default Digital Assistant, which may be impractical for a third-party app.

Real-world battery drain numbers for a microphone foreground service with PARTIAL_WAKE_LOCK running 8+ hours continuously -- benchmarks exist for location services but not specifically for continuous audio recording scenarios.
Specific Play Store review rejection rates for apps using REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS permission for always-on recording use cases versus using only the manual settings intent.
Pixel-specific behavior differences from AOSP baseline for Doze and foreground service management -- Pixel devices may be more permissive than Samsung/Xiaomi but documentation is sparse.
